Styles › Art Deco

1920s – 1940s · France → USA → Global

Art Deco

Glamorous, geometric, decorative — strong lines and symmetry.

  • Overview
  • Design Philosophy
  • Visual Characteristics
  • Materials
  • Color Palette
  • Façade Guide
  • Lighting
  • Building Types
  • Design Checklist
  • Common Mistakes
  • AI Prompts
  • Gallery
  • Related

01 — Overview

Overview

Definition. Art Deco is a decorative modern style marrying streamlined geometry with stylised ornament — stepped, symmetrical, vertical exteriors celebrating progress, speed and luxury.

Historical background. Named for the 1925 Paris Exposition, Deco swept from luxury goods to the great stepped skyscrapers of 1930s America.

Origin. Paris 1925; flourished in New York, Miami and worldwide.

Timeline

1925

Paris Exposition gives the style its name

1930

Chrysler Building completed

1931

Empire State Building tops out

1939

Streamline Moderne peaks

Key Architects

William Van AlenRaymond HoodEly Jacques KahnClaud Beelman

Influential Projects

Chrysler Building

William Van Alen · 1930
New York, USA

Empire State Building

Shreve, Lamb & Harmon · 1931
New York, USA

Eastern Columbia Building

Claud Beelman · 1930
Los Angeles, USA

02 — Design Philosophy

Design Philosophy

Core Principles

Modernity through ornamentVerticality & aspirationSymmetry & orderLuxury materialsStylised geometry

The Thinking

Deco disciplines decoration into geometric, machine-inspired motifs, expressing optimism and the romance of technology through controlled ornament and soaring form.

Values It Represents

GlamourProgressAspirationCraftsmanship

Why It Emerged

The prosperity and technological exuberance of the 1920s — to look modern and luxurious at once.

03 — Visual Characteristics

Visual Characteristics

MassingStepped, setback towers tapering to an ornamental crown; strong vertical thrust.
Form LanguageGeometric, symmetrical, ziggurat profiles.
CompositionTripartite base–shaft–crown with emphatic vertical piers.
ProportionTall and slender; ornament at base and summit.
GeometryZigzags, chevrons, sunbursts, stepped forms.
RhythmStrong vertical fluting and decorative bands.
SymmetryHighly symmetrical and axial.
ScaleMonumental and theatrical at the crown.
Roof TypesOrnamental stepped crowns, spires and decorative parapets.
Window ProportionVertical strips emphasised by raised piers and spandrel ornament.
EntranceGrand, symmetrical, lavishly decorated portal.
Façade ArticulationVertical piers with decorative metal/terracotta spandrels and carved geometric motifs.

04 — Materials

Materials

Polished stone & marble

Conveys luxury at the base.

Stainless steel / chrome

Gleaming machine-age crowns and trim.

Glazed terracotta

Colourful, repeatable cast ornament.

Bronze & brass

Rich metalwork for portals and grilles.

05 — Color Palette

Color Palette

Opulent, theatrical, confident — high contrast with metallic glamour.

Primary

Cream Stone
#e6dac6
Black
#1d1b18

Accent

Gold
#c8a14a
Jade
#2f7a6a
Deco Teal
#1f5f6b

Material Colors

Chrome
#b9bfc2
Bronze
#9c7b40

Typical Combinations

  • +Black + gold + cream
  • +Jade + chrome + black
  • +Coral + teal (Miami)

06 — Façade Design Guide

Façade Design Guide

Window CompositionGroup vertically between raised piers; emphasise the upward line.
EmphasisStrongly vertical — continuous piers to an ornamental crown.
Shading DevicesRecessed spandrels; ornament doubles as relief and shadow.
BalconiesRare; decorative metal railings with geometric patterns.
FramesMetal frames with chevron or sunburst detail.
OpeningsTall, narrow, repeated, sometimes stepped heads.
Architectural DetailsChevrons, zigzags, sunbursts, fluting, stylised motifs.
OrnamentationEssential and celebrated — geometric, symmetrical, at base, entrance and crown.

07 — Lighting

Lighting

Natural Lighting Strategy

Reflective stone and metal animate daylight; deep ornament casts crisp shadow.

Artificial Lighting Concept

Hidden cove lighting, neon accents and illuminated crowns.

Night Façade Appearance

Floodlit setbacks and a glowing metallic crown; theatrical drama.

Recommendations

Warm gold light on stone; accent crown and entrance; tasteful neon.

08 — Typical Building Types

Typical Building Types

Where this style performs best:

High-rise towersCinemas & theatresHotelsCivic buildingsDepartment storesTerminals

09 — Design Checklist

Design Checklist

Tap an item to mark it complete — saved to your browser.

  • Compose a stepped, setback tower with ornamental crown
  • Emphasise verticality with continuous piers
  • Organise base–shaft–crown
  • Add geometric ornament (chevrons, sunbursts)
  • Use luxury materials — marble, bronze, chrome
  • Design a grand symmetrical entrance
  • Use rich high-contrast colour with metallics
  • Keep ornament stylised and geometric

10 — Common Mistakes

Common Mistakes

Elements that contradict the style — what to avoid:

  • ✕Random or asymmetrical composition
  • ✕Organic Art-Nouveau ornament instead of geometric
  • ✕Flat unadorned façades with no crown/base
  • ✕Cheap finishes undercutting the luxury
  • ✕Over-ornamenting every surface

11 — AI Prompt Examples

AI Prompt Examples

Optimised for Midjourney, ChatGPT image tools or Claude — exterior renders. Tap copy to use.

Exterior

Art Deco skyscraper exterior, stepped setback tower, vertical limestone piers, ornamental stainless-steel sunburst crown, golden hour, monumental, dramatic upward angle, architectural photography --ar 2:3 --style raw

Façade Concept

Art Deco façade, geometric chevron and sunburst ornament, vertical fluting, glazed terracotta spandrels, bronze entrance, symmetrical elevation, high detail

Night View

Art Deco tower crown illuminated at night, glowing chrome sunburst, floodlit setbacks, neon accents, glamorous architectural night photography

Aerial View

Aerial of an Art Deco district, stepped towers with ornamental crowns, symmetrical boulevards, golden light, drone photography

Street Perspective

Street view of an Art Deco cinema exterior, grand symmetrical entrance, vertical neon sign, marble and bronze, 1930s glamour, documentary photography

Masterplan

Art Deco masterplan, axial symmetrical boulevards, stepped towers, ceremonial plaza, ornamental geometry, gold-and-black palette

Modern

Clean lines, functional forms, no ornament.

Europe → GlobalRational

Contemporary

Current trends, evolving — mixed materials, clean geometry.

GlobalCurrent

Minimalism

Simplicity — 'less is more'.
